title: Monthly Win-Loss Review
summary: A monthly system for reviewing closed-won and closed-lost deals so the sales motion improves from evidence instead of anecdotes.
inputs: Closed-won and closed-lost deals from the month
Call notes and recordings from material deals
outputs: Pattern list for objections, pricing, ICP fit, and deal control
Coaching actions for the next month

steps.title: Review the deal outcomes
steps.description: List every meaningful closed-won and closed-lost deal from the month with size, segment, source, and stage progression.

steps.title: Identify the real reasons
steps.description: Separate stated reasons from likely true reasons. Check for recurring patterns such as pricing, poor qualification, weak urgency, competitor fit, or process breakdown.

steps.title: Group the patterns
steps.description: Cluster findings into themes like ICP mismatch, discovery quality, proposal quality, negotiation weakness, or procurement friction.

steps.title: Turn the patterns into changes
steps.description: Choose the one to three changes that should affect qualification, messaging, enablement, or pipeline process next month.

kpis.target: 90%+ of meaningful losses tagged with a credible primary reason
At least one concrete messaging, qualification, or process change applied each cycle

whenToImplement: As soon as there is enough monthly deal volume that the team can learn from patterns rather than isolated stories.
delegationNotes: A sales leader or revenue operator can prepare the analysis later, but the founder should stay close while founder-led sales is still shaping the motion.
automationNotes: Use CRM stage hygiene, loss reason fields, call recording summaries, and simple tagging workflows to reduce manual analysis time.
